sections.instruction: Use the standard nav when the template needs the clearest service, area, phone, and booking paths.
sections.name: Primary navigation

sections.instruction: Use a split hero with a bounded fixed-ratio image frame instead of a full-screen image.
sections.name: Fixed-ratio split image

sections.instruction: Use one dominant narrative idea with four concise supporting points on a 14-column grid. Keep the lead idea interpretive and the supporting cards evidence-based.
sections.name: Main idea support grid

sections.instruction: Use a large title-only section header when the next content module needs a stronger visual reset.
sections.name: Large section header

sections.instruction: Use two detailed decision cards on a 14-column grid. Each card should contain a focused header, two concise paragraphs, a short evidence list, and a bottom-aligned next-step link.
sections.name: Split decision large

sections.instruction: Use the current conversion band when the page needs a polished direct next step.
sections.name: CTA

sections.instruction: Use current process steps when the page needs a clearer, more styled decision sequence.
sections.name: Process steps

sections.instruction: Use a focused project gallery when one image and concise project details should work together as a mini case study.
sections.name: Project case study gallery

sections.instruction: Include only the questions that affect whether someone contacts you.
sections.name: FAQ

sections.instruction: Begin the request flow in-page with system and service choices, then carry those answers into step two of the request modal.
sections.name: Contact section modal begin

sections.instruction: End with service links, areas, contact details, and legal links.
sections.name: Footer

sections.instruction: Use the standard nav when the template needs the clearest service, area, phone, and booking paths.
sections.name: Primary navigation

sections.instruction: Lead with direct copy first, then use the image below to keep the page useful and quick to understand.
sections.name: Content top image bottom

sections.instruction: Use scrolling logo proof for affiliations, certifications, training, manufacturer badges, or partner networks.
sections.name: Logo marquee

sections.instruction: Use natural longform paragraphs with short sticky bullets and a context-specific side-list label. Do not use generic Important ideas copy.
sections.name: Sticky ideas content

sections.instruction: Use a mixed-size horizontal photo gallery when people, projects, or proof images should carry the visual story.
sections.name: Photo gallery carousel

sections.instruction: Use a large editorial interstitial thought. A hard return creates a separate animated paragraph; do not treat it as standard body or card copy.
sections.name: Reveal paragraph

sections.instruction: Use an asymmetrical intro and feature card cluster when a why-choose-us section needs scannable proof points.
sections.name: Asymmetric feature cards

sections.instruction: Use a compact two-card comparison when a homeowner needs help deciding whether repair or replacement makes more sense after inspection.
sections.name: Split decision

sections.instruction: Use a headline with a scrolling banner when proof points should move beside a stronger editorial claim.
sections.name: Headline with Scrolling Banner

sections.instruction: End with service links, areas, contact details, and legal links.
sections.name: Footer

templates.name: Home - full image hero, side scrolling service cards

sections.instruction: Use the floating nav when the hero needs a polished first-viewport frame.
sections.name: Floating bento navigation

sections.instruction: Use a strong image, calm h1, review proof, and one visible request path.
sections.name: Fullscreen image hero

sections.instruction: Use scrolling logo proof for affiliations, certifications, training, manufacturer badges, or partner networks.
sections.name: Logo marquee

sections.instruction: Use a service rail when there are more service paths than a small grid can handle gracefully.
sections.name: Scroll service cards

sections.instruction: Use varied quote lengths to create a fuller body of evidence.
sections.name: Masonry testimonials

sections.instruction: Keep a promise fixed while supporting details move through response, diagnosis, options, and follow-up.
sections.name: Sticky card stream content

sections.instruction: Use a fixed-cover fade as an immersive, image-led closing conversion. Write backgroundTitle and backgroundBody for the full-screen closing message, then foregroundEyebrow, foregroundTitle, foregroundBody, and contactDetails for the request path. Do not leave backgroundTitle or backgroundBody blank.
sections.name: Fixed cover fade

sections.instruction: End with service links, areas, contact details, and legal links.
sections.name: Footer

templates.name: Home - split hero, trust marquee, service card grid

sections.instruction: Use the standard nav when the template needs the clearest service, area, phone, and booking paths.
sections.name: Primary navigation

sections.instruction: Use h1, one primary booking CTA, one services CTA, and three compact trust stats beside a full-bleed image column.
sections.name: Split content and full image

sections.instruction: Use short repeated claims when there are many small proof points.
sections.name: Trust marquee

sections.instruction: Use 6-9 bento-style service cards for a fuller service scan. The fixed layout rhythm is big small small, small small big, then big small small.
sections.name: Services bento cards

sections.instruction: Use two large three-column decision cards with a simple left-aligned header and link above them.
sections.name: Split large cards

sections.instruction: Use stacked feature cards when a why-choose-us section needs larger icon-led proof points with the same copy formula as asymmetric cards.
sections.name: Stacked feature cards

sections.instruction: Use when visitors need to confirm service coverage before starting a request.
sections.name: Service area zip lookup
